mainEntity.name: What if my neck injury means I can't do my physical labor job anymore?

acceptedAnswer.text: You may be eligible for retraining through vocational rehabilitation or permanent disability benefits if your neck injury prevents you from returning to your previous line of work. Your employer must accommodate your doctor's restrictions.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Unlike a back injury, a neck injury often affects your arms, hands, and grip strength. If you have a desk job, you might be able to return quickly with an ergonomic setup. If you are a laborer or driver in places like Boise or Salt Lake City, a neck fusion might permanently prevent you from safely operating heavy machinery or looking up/down repeatedly. Your doctor will provide 'Permanent Restrictions' that your employer must honor.

Key factors include the physical demands of your specific job and the degree of your range-of-motion loss.

If your boss tries to make you work a job that requires looking up (like overhead wiring) after a neck injury, you have the right to say no based on your medical restrictions.

If you can't return to your old career, we can help you fight for vocational rehabilitation benefits to train for a new one.
